description_en: The Battle of Buzakha was the first decisive engagement of the Riddah Wars, fought against Tulayha ibn Khuwaylid of the Banu Asad tribe who had declared himself a prophet after the death of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ. Khalid ibn al-Walid led the Muslim force north from Medina and routed Tulayha's forces. Tulayha fled to Syria and later accepted Islam, becoming a Muslim warrior. This victory broke the back of apostasy in northern Arabia and proved that the nascent caliphate under Abu Bakr would not allow the unravelling of the Islamic state.
sources: al-Tabari, History of the Prophets and Kings
description_id: Perang Buzakha adalah pertempuran penentu pertama dalam Perang Riddah, dilakukan untuk menumpas pemberontakan suku-suku Arab yang murtad setelah wafatnya Nabi ﷺ. Khalid ibn al-Walid memimpin pasukan Muslim melawan Thalihah ibn Khuwaylid si nabi palsu dari Bani Asad. Kemenangan di Buzakha menghancurkan kekuatan utama kemurtadan di Arabia tengah dan membuktikan keteguhan Khalifah Abu Bakar al-Shiddiq yang menolak kompromi apapun terhadap orang yang murtad atau menolak membayar zakat.

title_en: Battle of Yamama — Death of Musaylima
description_en: The Battle of Yamama was the bloodiest engagement of the Riddah Wars, fought against Musaylima al-Kadhdhab (the Liar), who had claimed prophethood among the Banu Hanifa in central Arabia. Khalid ibn al-Walid commanded a Muslim force against an army of approximately 40,000. The fighting was ferocious — 1,200 Muslims were martyred, including many Huffaz (memorisers of the Quran). Musaylima was killed by Wahshi ibn Harb, the same warrior who had martyred Hamza at Uhud. The scale of Quran-memoriser martyrdoms prompted Abu Bakr to order the compilation of the Quran into a single mushaf.
title_id: Perang Yamamah — Kematian Musaylimah si Pendusta
description_id: Perang Yamamah adalah pertempuran paling berdarah dalam Perang Riddah, dilakukan untuk menumpas Musaylimah al-Kadzdzab si nabi palsu dari Bani Hanifah di Najd. Sekitar 1.200 sahabat gugur sebagai syuhada termasuk banyak penghafal Al-Qur'an. Musaylimah akhirnya terbunuh oleh Wahshi ibn Harb — tombak yang sama yang pernah membunuh Hamzah di Uhud kini digunakan untuk membunuh musuh Islam. Besarnya korban jiwa para hafizh mendorong Abu Bakar menginisiasi pengumpulan mushaf Al-Qur'an pertama.

description_en: The Battle of Ullais was fought on the banks of the Euphrates as part of Khalid ibn al-Walid's Iraqi campaign. A combined Persian and Arab Christian force attempted to stop the Muslim advance into Sasanid-controlled Mesopotamia. Khalid employed a flanking strategy that cut off the enemy's retreat, resulting in a decisive Muslim victory. The battle opened the route into the heart of Iraq and demonstrated the vulnerability of the Sasanid frontier to the newly energised Muslim armies.
description_id: Perang Ullais adalah bagian dari serangkaian kemenangan cemerlang Khalid ibn al-Walid di front Irak. Di tepi Sungai Efrat, pasukan Muslim menghadapi gabungan pasukan Persia dan Arab Kristen. Kemenangan ini memantapkan kendali Muslim atas wilayah-wilayah strategis di selatan Mesopotamia dan membuka jalan menuju Hira, ibukota Arab yang berada di bawah lindungan Persia.

description_en: The Battle of al-Walaja was a significant engagement in southern Iraq where Khalid ibn al-Walid executed one of his famous double-envelopment manoeuvres. A large Persian force supported by Arab allies had positioned themselves expecting a frontal assault, but Khalid divided his army and attacked simultaneously from multiple directions, encircling the enemy. The victory cleared the way toward the Sasanid provincial capital and further eroded Persian ability to mount coordinated resistance in Iraq.
opponent: Sasanid Persians and Bakr ibn Wa'il allies
description_id: Perang al-Walaja adalah kemenangan signifikan Khalid ibn al-Walid dalam kampanye Irak selatan. Khalid menggunakan taktik pengepungan ganda yang brilian untuk menghancurkan pasukan Persia yang lebih besar. Kemenangan demi kemenangan ini semakin melemahkan kemampuan Kekaisaran Sasaniyah untuk mempertahankan wilayah Arab mereka dan mempercepat laju penaklukan Islam di Mesopotamia.

description_en: The Battle of Ajnadayn was one of the first major Muslim victories in Syria-Palestine, fought between a unified Muslim army and a large Byzantine force. Khalid ibn al-Walid had made his legendary march from Iraq to Syria to take command. The battle was a decisive Muslim victory that broke Byzantine control over southern Palestine. Abu Bakr died shortly after hearing news of the victory. The victory opened the way for the subsequent conquests of Damascus, Jerusalem, and all of the Levant.
muslim_commander: Khalid ibn al-Walid (after forced march from Iraq)
description_id: Perang Ajnadayn adalah salah satu kemenangan Muslim pertama yang besar atas pasukan Byzantium di wilayah Suriah-Palestina. Khalid ibn al-Walid memimpin pasukan Muslim dalam pertempuran yang mengalahkan tentara Byzantium yang lebih besar. Kemenangan ini membuka pintu bagi penaklukan Suriah dan Palestina secara besar-besaran dan membuktikan bahwa Islam mampu mengalahkan dua kekuatan adidaya dunia sekaligus — Persia dan Byzantium.

description_en: The Battle of the Bridge was a rare Muslim defeat during the Iraqi campaigns. Abu Ubayd al-Thaqafi commanded a Muslim force that crossed a pontoon bridge over the Euphrates to engage the Persians on their own ground, against the tactical advice of his officers. The Persians deployed war elephants that terrified the Muslim horses. Abu Ubayd himself was killed attempting to hamstring an elephant. The Muslim rearguard managed an orderly retreat across the bridge, but the defeat temporarily halted the Iraqi advance and demonstrated the dangers of engaging Persian war elephants without preparation.
description_id: Perang Jembatan adalah salah satu kekalahan langka Muslim dalam periode penaklukan. Abu Ubayd al-Thaqafi memimpin pasukan Muslim melintasi jembatan ke wilayah Persia, bertentangan dengan strategi yang lebih bijak. Gajah-gajah perang Persia membuat panik pasukan Muslim, dan Abu Ubayd gugur. Kekalahan ini menjadi pelajaran tentang pentingnya strategi yang tepat, dan pasukan Muslim segera bangkit kembali dengan mengalahkan Persia di Buwayb.

description_en: The Battle of al-Buwayb was the Muslim answer to the defeat at the Bridge, fought under al-Muthanna ibn Haritha al-Shaybani. The Muslims lured a Persian force across the Euphrates, then cut their line of retreat by destroying the bridge behind them. The resulting encirclement led to a decisive Muslim victory and restored morale after the disaster at al-Jisr. Al-Muthanna showed exceptional tactical skill in reversing the momentum of the campaign, though he was himself wounded and died shortly after.
description_id: Perang al-Buwayb adalah jawaban pasukan Muslim atas kekalahan di Perang Jembatan. Al-Muthanna ibn Harithah memimpin pasukan Muslim membalas dendam dengan kemenangan gemilang atas pasukan Persia yang lebih besar. Pertempuran ini memulihkan semangat tempur Muslim di front Irak dan membuka jalan bagi penaklukan lebih lanjut yang berujung pada kemenangan besar di Qadisiyyah.

description_en: The siege of Damascus lasted several months as Muslim forces under Khalid ibn al-Walid and Abu Ubayda ibn al-Jarrah encircled the ancient city. Various Muslim commanders positioned themselves at the city's different gates. When Khalid stormed one gate by force, the defenders at another gate were simultaneously negotiating a peace treaty with Abu Ubayda. Damascus was thus taken partly by force and partly by treaty, giving rise to differing rulings on the status of its inhabitants. The conquest of Syria's greatest city announced Muslim power throughout the Levant.
muslim_commander: Khalid ibn al-Walid and Abu Ubayda ibn al-Jarrah
description_id: Pengepungan Damaskus berlangsung selama beberapa bulan dengan pasukan Muslim mengepung dari berbagai sisi kota kuno ini. Ketika kota akhirnya jatuh, terjadi situasi unik: satu bagian kota menyerah melalui perjanjian damai kepada Khalid ibn al-Walid dari arah timur, sementara Abu Ubaydah ibn al-Jarrah memasuki dari arah barat melalui pertempuran. Keduanya bertemu di tengah kota dan menyepakati perlakuan damai bagi seluruh penduduk. Damaskus kemudian menjadi ibukota pertama Dinasti Umayyah.

description_en: The Battle of Yarmouk was one of the most consequential battles in world history, deciding the fate of the entire Levant. Emperor Heraclius sent a massive Byzantine army of 100,000–150,000 soldiers to drive the Muslims back. The Muslim force numbered around 25,000–40,000 under Abu Ubayda ibn al-Jarrah, with Khalid ibn al-Walid commanding the mobile reserve. After six days of battle, the Byzantines were routed and driven into the Yarmouk River gorge. The battle ended Byzantine power in the Levant permanently. Upon hearing the news, Emperor Heraclius bid Syria farewell: 'Peace be upon you, O Syria — what a wonderful country you are for the enemy.'
muslim_commander: Abu Ubayda ibn al-Jarrah (Khalid as field commander)
description_id: Perang Yarmuk adalah salah satu pertempuran paling menentukan dalam sejarah dunia, terjadi pada 15 H / 636 M. Pasukan Muslim sekitar 24.000-40.000 orang di bawah komando Abu Ubaydah ibn al-Jarrah dan Khalid ibn al-Walid menghadapi tentara Byzantium yang berjumlah jauh lebih besar di tepi Sungai Yarmuk. Setelah pertempuran selama enam hari yang melelahkan, pasukan Muslim meraih kemenangan gemilang. Kekalahan ini mengakhiri kekuasaan Byzantium atas Suriah dan Palestina selamanya.

description_en: The Battle of al-Qadisiyyah was the decisive engagement that broke Sasanid Persian power in Iraq and opened all of Mesopotamia to the Muslims. Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas commanded a Muslim army of approximately 30,000. The Sasanid commander Rustam Farrokhzad led a force with war elephants across the Euphrates. The battle lasted three days and nights, and on the fourth day the Muslims found an opening: the wind turned, blinding the Persians with dust. Rustam was killed and his banner fell, breaking the Persian morale. The white palace of Ctesiphon lay open before the Muslim army.
description_id: Perang al-Qadisiyyah pada 15-17 H / 636-637 M adalah pertempuran yang menghancurkan Kekaisaran Sasaniyah Persia selamanya. Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas memimpin pasukan Muslim menghadapi tentara Persia yang besar dengan gajah-gajah perang di bawah panglima besar Rustam Farrokhzad. Setelah pertempuran selama empat hari, Rustam terbunuh dan pasukan Persia hancur total. Kemenangan ini membuka seluruh Mesopotamia bagi Islam dan menjadi titik balik definitif dalam sejarah Timur Tengah.

description_en: After Qadisiyyah, Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas advanced on Ctesiphon, the Sasanid imperial capital and one of the greatest cities of the ancient world. The Muslim army crossed the Tigris and entered the city largely unopposed — the Sasanid Emperor Yazdegerd III had fled with the treasury. The Muslims captured the legendary White Palace (Taq Kasra), whose great arch still stands today as one of the largest single-span brick arches ever built. Sa'd led the first Friday prayer within its hall. The immense spoils, including the famous Persian carpet and the crown jewels, were sent to Umar in Medina.
description_id: Setelah Qadisiyyah, Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas maju menuju Ktesifon, ibukota Kekaisaran Sasaniyah yang megah. Kaisar Yazdgerd III telah melarikan diri, meninggalkan kota kosong. Pasukan Muslim memasuki istana Khosrow yang legendaris dan menemukan kemewahan yang belum pernah mereka lihat. Sa'd memimpin shalat Shubuh di aula utama istana. Harta yang ditemukan dibagi-bagikan dan seperlima dikirim ke Madinah. Kejatuhan Ktesifon menandai runtuhnya pusat Kekaisaran Sasaniyah.

description_en: The surrender of Jerusalem was one of the most momentous events in Islamic history. Patriarch Sophronius agreed to surrender the city but insisted the keys be handed only to the Caliph himself. Umar ibn al-Khattab made the journey personally from Medina to receive the keys of Jerusalem — arriving on camelback, dressed in simple clothing, alternating the ride with his servant. His treaty with the inhabitants, known as the Covenant of Umar, guaranteed their lives, property, and religious freedom. Umar refused to pray inside the Church of the Holy Sepulchre, praying outside so that Muslims would not later claim the church as a mosque. He then visited and prayed at the ruins of Masjid al-Aqsa.
muslim_commander: Abu Ubayda ibn al-Jarrah (Umar personally received surrender)
description_id: Penyerahan Yerusalem adalah salah satu momen paling bersejarah dalam Islam. Patriark Sophronius bersikeras menyerahkan kota hanya kepada Khalifah Umar ibn al-Khattab secara langsung. Umar datang dari Madinah dengan penuh kerendahan hati, naik unta bergantian dengan pembantunya. Beliau menandatangani al-'Uhdah al-Umariyyah yang menjamin keselamatan jiwa, harta, dan tempat ibadah semua penduduk. Ketika tiba waktu shalat di dalam Gereja Makam Kudus, Umar menolak shalat di sana agar tidak dijadikan alasan mengubahnya menjadi masjid.

description_en: The Battle of Jalula took place shortly after Qadisiyyah, as Sasanid forces regrouped in the foothills east of the Tigris to block further Muslim advances into Persia. Hashim ibn Utba led the Muslim force. The Persians had fortified a strong defensive position, but the Muslims persisted through months of siege-like attrition before a decisive engagement scattered the Persian army. Jalula opened the road into the Persian heartland, and Muslim forces pursued the remnants toward Khurasan and what is now Iran.
description_id: Perang Jalula terjadi tidak lama setelah Qadisiyyah, ketika sisa-sisa pasukan Persia Sasaniyah mencoba melakukan perlawanan terakhir di benteng Jalula di tepi Sungai Diyala. Hashim ibn Utbah memimpin pasukan Muslim dalam pertempuran yang memaksa sisa pasukan Persia mundur ke Hulwan di kaki pegunungan Zagros. Kemenangan ini membuka pintu menuju dataran tinggi Iran dan mempercepat runtuhnya Kekaisaran Sasaniyah.

description_en: Umar ordered Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas to establish Kufa as a garrison city and administrative centre for the Iraqi campaigns. Located on the western bank of the Euphrates near ancient Babylon, Kufa was laid out in a grid with the great mosque at its centre. It rapidly became one of the most important cities in the Muslim world — a centre of Islamic learning, the seat of Ali ibn Abi Talib's caliphate, and later a home to major juristic and theological schools. The founding of Kufa and Basra transformed the character of the Iraqi conquest from a military campaign into a permanent settlement.
description_id: Khalifah Umar memerintahkan Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas mendirikan Kufah sebagai kota garnisun dan pusat administrasi di Irak. Kufah dibangun di tepi Sungai Efrat sebagai kota yang dirancang khusus untuk pasukan Muslim dan keluarga mereka, memisahkan mereka dari penduduk setempat untuk menjaga kemurnian akidah. Kufah kemudian berkembang menjadi salah satu pusat keilmuan Islam terpenting dan menjadi pusat Kekhalifahan Ali ibn Abi Talib.

description_en: Utba ibn Ghazwan established Basra as the southern garrison city for the Iraqi campaigns, at the confluence of the Tigris and Euphrates near the Persian Gulf. Like Kufa in the north, Basra was designed as a base for ongoing military operations and a centre for Islamic governance. It grew into a major port, a hub of Arab tribal settlement, and one of the early centres of Arabic grammar and poetry. Basra would later be significant as the base of operations during the first Fitna.
description_id: Basra didirikan oleh Utbah ibn Ghazwan sebagai kota garnisun selatan untuk kampanye Irak, mirip dengan Kufah di utara. Terletak di pertemuan Sungai Tigris dan Efrat, Basra segera berkembang menjadi pelabuhan penting dan pusat perdagangan. Kota ini kemudian menjadi salah satu pusat ilmu dan sastra Arab terbesar, tempat lahirnya berbagai cabang ilmu bahasa Arab dan menjadi saingan intelektual Kufah dalam berbagai bidang keilmuan.

description_en: The Battle of Heliopolis was the decisive engagement in the Muslim conquest of Egypt. Amr ibn al-As had led a Muslim force of roughly 4,000 into Egypt with Umar's cautious blessing, and received reinforcements including Zubayr ibn al-Awwam. The Byzantine garrison of Egypt, led by the Patriarch Cyrus and the general Theodore, was defeated decisively near ancient Heliopolis (modern Ain Shams, near Cairo). The battle opened the Nile Delta to Muslim control and began the end of centuries of Byzantine rule over Egypt, one of the richest provinces of the empire.
opponent: Byzantine garrison of Egypt — general Theodore
description_id: Perang Heliopolis adalah pertempuran penentu dalam penaklukan Muslim atas Mesir. Amr ibn al-As memimpin pasukan Muslim sekitar 15.000 orang melawan pasukan Byzantium yang jauh lebih besar di dekat Kairo modern. Kemenangan telak ini menghancurkan kemampuan Byzantium mempertahankan Mesir dan membuka lembah Nil bagi Islam. Penaklukan Mesir yang kaya raya memberikan sumber daya luar biasa bagi ekspansi Islam lebih lanjut ke Afrika Utara.

description_en: After Heliopolis, Amr ibn al-As besieged Alexandria — one of the greatest cities of the ancient world and the intellectual capital of the eastern Mediterranean. The city held out for months behind its formidable sea walls, supported by the Byzantine navy. Eventually a negotiated surrender was reached: the Byzantines were permitted to evacuate by sea, and the city was taken without destruction. Amr established al-Fustat (near modern Cairo) as the new administrative capital of Egypt rather than Alexandria, a decision that shaped the country's geography to this day. Egypt's entry into the Islamic world transformed both the country and the wider umma.
description_id: Setelah Heliopolis, Amr ibn al-As mengepung Aleksandria — salah satu kota terbesar dan terkaya di dunia kuno, pusat peradaban Hellenistik dan Kristen Koptik. Meski kota ini sangat kuat, pengepungan berlangsung selama beberapa bulan sebelum akhirnya menyerah melalui negosiasi. Perjanjian yang ditandatangani menjamin keselamatan penduduk dan kebebasan menjalankan agama mereka. Penaklukan Aleksandria melengkapi penguasaan Muslim atas seluruh Mesir.

description_en: The Battle of Nihawand, called by Muslim historians 'the Victory of Victories' (Fath al-Futuh), was the final major battle against the Sasanid Empire. Emperor Yazdegerd III had assembled a last great Sasanid army in western Persia. Al-Nu'man ibn Muqarrin commanded the Muslim force; he was martyred in battle but his successor Hudhayfah ibn al-Yaman pressed the attack until the Sasanid army was destroyed. The Emperor fled and was eventually killed near Merv in 651 CE. With Nihawand, organised Sasanid resistance ended and Persia lay open to Muslim settlement and eventual conversion. The event effectively marked the end of the Sasanid Empire after more than four centuries.
muslim_commander: al-Nu'man ibn Muqarrin (martyred), then Hudhayfah ibn al-Yaman
description_id: Perang Nihawand, disebut para sejarawan Muslim sebagai 'Fath al-Futuh' (Penaklukan Segala Penaklukan), adalah pertempuran akhir yang menghancurkan Kekaisaran Sasaniyah. Nu'man ibn Muqarrin memimpin pasukan Muslim menghadapi tentara Persia terakhir yang besar. Nu'man gugur sebagai syahid dalam pertempuran ini, namun pasukannya meraih kemenangan telak. Setelah Nihawand, Persia tidak mampu lagi mengorganisir perlawanan besar, dan seluruh dataran tinggi Iran segera berada di bawah kendali Muslim.

description_en: Umar ibn al-Khattab was stabbed by Abu Lu'lu'ah Firuz, a Persian slave of al-Mughira ibn Shu'ba, while leading the Fajr prayer in Masjid al-Nabawi. Abu Lu'lu'ah attacked with a double-bladed dagger, wounding multiple worshippers before being subdued. Umar died three days later from his wounds. Dying, he refused to designate his own successor and instead appointed a shura council of six senior companions to choose the next caliph. Umar's decade of rule had transformed the Muslim world: he established the Hijri calendar, the diwan (state treasury and register), the judicial system, and oversaw the conquest of Persia, Iraq, Syria, Palestine, and Egypt.
description_id: Umar ibn al-Khattab رضي الله عنه, Khalifah kedua Islam, ditikam oleh Abu Lu'lu'ah Firuz, seorang budak Persia milik al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah, saat sedang memimpin shalat Shubuh di masjid Madinah pada 26 Dzulhijjah 23 H / November 644 M. Abu Lu'lu'ah menyerangnya dengan pisau bermata dua. Umar bertahan beberapa hari sebelum wafat. Sebelum wafat, ia menunjuk enam orang untuk bermusyawarah memilih khalifah berikutnya. Wafatnya Umar adalah musibah besar bagi umat Islam.

description_en: The conquest of Cyprus was the first major Muslim naval operation in the Mediterranean, ordered by Uthman ibn Affan and commanded by Muawiyah ibn Abi Sufyan, the governor of Syria. A fleet assembled from Syrian ports crossed to Cyprus, which had been used as a Byzantine naval base. The island submitted to Muslim authority after negotiations, agreeing to pay tribute. The campaign demonstrated the emergence of Muslim sea power and ended the exclusively Byzantine control of the eastern Mediterranean. Cyprus would later be raided again and eventually established as a shared Muslim-Byzantine protectorate.
description_id: Penaklukan Siprus adalah operasi angkatan laut besar pertama dalam sejarah Islam, dilaksanakan pada masa Khalifah Utsman ibn Affan. Muawiyah ibn Abi Sufyan, gubernur Suriah, memimpin armada besar ke Siprus — sebuah pulau strategis yang selama ini menjadi basis operasi angkatan laut Byzantium. Ekspedisi ini membuktikan kemampuan Islam berkembang menjadi kekuatan laut, tidak hanya kekuatan darat.

description_en: The Battle of the Masts was the first great Muslim naval battle and one of the most significant sea battles in the medieval Mediterranean. Emperor Constans II personally led a Byzantine fleet of reportedly 500–1,000 ships against a Muslim fleet of 200 ships under Abd Allah ibn Abi Sarh. The two fleets lashed their ships together to fight as on land — hence 'Battle of the Masts.' The Muslims won a decisive victory. Emperor Constans fled to Sicily, where he was later assassinated. The battle established Muslim naval supremacy in the eastern Mediterranean and opened the way for future campaigns against Constantinople itself.
description_id: Perang Dzat al-Shawari (Perang Tiang-Tiang Layar) adalah pertempuran laut besar pertama dalam sejarah Islam, di mana armada Muslim mengalahkan armada Byzantium yang dipimpin Kaisar Constantius II sendiri. Terjadi di perairan Mediterania pada 34 H / 655 M, pertempuran ini membuktikan bahwa kekuatan Islam tidak terbatas di darat saja. Kemenangan ini memberikan kendali Muslim atas Mediterania Timur.

description_en: Uthman ibn Affan commissioned the standardisation of the Quran into a single authoritative written form. Hudhayfah ibn al-Yaman had reported to Uthman that differences in Quranic recitation were causing disputes among Muslim soldiers from different regions. Uthman convened a committee under Zayd ibn Thabit — who had led the original compilation under Abu Bakr — to produce a master copy based on Abu Bakr's mushaf and confirmed against the recollections of companions who had memorised the Quran directly from the Prophet ﷺ. Copies were sent to the major cities and all other written versions were ordered destroyed. The Uthmanic Codex is the direct ancestor of every Quran in existence today, preserved unchanged for over 1,400 years.
description_id: Khalifah Utsman ibn Affan memerintahkan penyatuan Al-Qur'an ke dalam satu mushaf standar yang resmi — dikenal sebagai Mushaf Utsmani. Perbedaan dialek bacaan di berbagai wilayah telah menimbulkan kebingungan, mendorong Utsman mengambil tindakan tegas. Zayd ibn Tsabit dan sekelompok sahabat mengerjakan tugas mulia ini. Beberapa salinan mushaf standar dikirimkan ke berbagai wilayah, dan salinan-salinan yang berbeda diperintahkan untuk dibakar. Ini adalah salah satu kontribusi terbesar Utsman bagi Islam.

description_en: Uthman ibn Affan was besieged in his home in Medina by rebel groups from Egypt, Kufa, and Basra, who accused his administration of nepotism and misrule. After weeks of siege — during which he refused offers of armed rescue to avoid Muslim bloodshed — rebels broke into his house while he was reading the Quran. He was killed, his blood falling on the mushaf open before him. The assassination of Uthman was the original wound of the first Fitna, splitting the Muslim community in a way that shaped Islamic politics for centuries. The question of his blood — who was responsible, who failed to protect him, and who would avenge him — drove the subsequent wars between Ali, Aisha, and Muawiyah.
description_id: Utsman ibn Affan رضي الله عنه, Khalifah ketiga Islam, dikepung di rumahnya di Madinah oleh kelompok-kelompok pemberontak dari Mesir, Irak, dan tempat lain yang mengklaim berbagai ketidakpuasan terhadap pemerintahannya. Setelah pengepungan selama sekitar empat puluh hari, para pemberontak menyerbu masuk dan membunuhnya saat beliau sedang membaca Al-Qur'an pada 18 Dzulhijjah 35 H / Juni 656 M. Pembunuhan Utsman membuka babak kegelapan Fitnah Pertama yang memecah belah umat Islam.

description_en: The Battle of the Camel was the first armed conflict between Muslim factions, fought near Basra. Aisha (the mother of the believers), Talha ibn Ubaydullah, and Zubayr ibn al-Awwam led a force demanding justice for the blood of Uthman and opposing Ali's caliphate. Ali had marched from Medina to Kufa and then toward Basra seeking reconciliation, but negotiations broke down. The battle was fierce: Talha and Zubayr were killed (Zubayr departing the battle before being killed away from it), and Aisha directed affairs from a fortified howdah atop a camel — giving the battle its name. Ali's forces prevailed. He honoured Aisha and sent her safely back to Medina. The battle deeply scarred the Muslim conscience and established the theological precedent that companions could err in political matters.
opponent: Aisha, Talha, and Zubayr ibn al-Awwam
description_id: Perang Jamal adalah konflik bersenjata pertama antara sesama Muslim, terjadi pada Jumadil Akhir 36 H / 656 M di Basra. Di satu pihak adalah Khalifah Ali ibn Abi Talib, dan di pihak lain adalah Thalhah ibn Ubaidillah, Zubair ibn al-Awwam, dan Aisyah RA yang menuntut penyelidikan pembunuhan Utsman. Pertempuran ini dinamai 'Perang Unta' karena Aisyah berada di tandu di atas unta. Thalhah dan Zubair terbunuh; Aisyah ditahan dengan penuh hormat dan diantar kembali ke Madinah oleh Ali.

description_en: The Battle of Siffin was fought between Ali ibn Abi Talib and Muawiyah ibn Abi Sufyan on the banks of the Euphrates in Syria over several months of skirmishing and one major engagement. Muawiyah, as governor of Syria, had refused to give allegiance to Ali until the killers of Uthman were punished. When Ali's forces were gaining the upper hand in the decisive encounter, Muawiyah's advisor Amr ibn al-As ordered soldiers to hoist copies of the Quran on their spears, calling for arbitration. Many of Ali's followers compelled him to accept arbitration — a decision he later regretted. The arbitration at Adhruh produced no clear result, and the conflict remained unresolved. Siffin permanently divided the community into factions that would shape the trajectory of Islamic history.
opponent: Muawiyah ibn Abi Sufyan, governor of Syria
description_id: Perang Shiffin adalah pertempuran terbesar dalam Fitnah Pertama, terjadi di tepi Sungai Efrat antara pasukan Khalifah Ali ibn Abi Talib dan Muawiyah ibn Abi Sufyan, gubernur Suriah yang menuntut pembalasan atas kematian Utsman. Pertempuran berlangsung berhari-hari dengan korban besar di kedua pihak. Ketika pasukan Muawiyah mulai terdesak, Amr ibn al-As memerintahkan mengangkat mushaf Al-Qur'an sebagai isyarat arbitrase. Ali menerima, memicu perpecahan di pihaknya sendiri.

description_en: The Battle of Nahrawan was Ali ibn Abi Talib's decisive campaign against the Khawarij — a puritanical faction that had broken away from his army after Siffin, declaring that accepting arbitration was disbelief. The Khawarij had established themselves along the Nahrawan Canal east of the Tigris and were terrorising the population, declaring all who disagreed with them to be apostates worthy of death. Ali confronted them, offering them return to the community; only a small number accepted. In the subsequent battle, the Khawarij were nearly annihilated. However, scattered survivors fled and would later take revenge by assassinating Ali — and Nahrawan entrenched the Khawarij conviction that Ali was an apostate.
description_id: Perang Nahrawan adalah kampanye Ali ibn Abi Talib melawan kaum Khawarij — kelompok yang semula adalah pendukungnya namun kemudian memisahkan diri dan mengkafirkan Ali serta siapa pun yang menerima arbitrase. Kaum Khawarij berkumpul di Nahrawan dan terus melakukan pembunuhan terhadap siapa pun yang berbeda pandangan. Ali membubarkan mereka secara militer dengan kemenangan telak, membunuh sebagian besar. Namun sisa-sisa Khawarij terus menjadi ancaman dan salah satu dari mereka akhirnya membunuh Ali.

title_en: Assassination of Ali ibn Abi Talib
description_en: Ali ibn Abi Talib was struck with a poison-coated sword by Abd al-Rahman ibn Muljam al-Muradi, a Khawarij avenger for Nahrawan, while leading the Fajr prayer in the mosque of Kufa. Ibn Muljam had conspired with two others who simultaneously attempted to assassinate Muawiyah in Damascus and Amr ibn al-As in Egypt — both failed. Ali survived two days before dying from the wound, his last words urging his sons toward mercy and moderation. His death ended the era of the Rashidun Caliphs, all four of whom had been close companions of the Prophet ﷺ, and three of whom died by assassination. Ali is regarded by Sunni Muslims as the last of the four Rightly-Guided Caliphs and one of the ten companions promised Paradise.
description_id: Ali ibn Abi Talib رضي الله عنه, Khalifah keempat dan sepupu sekaligus menantu Nabi ﷺ, ditikam dengan pedang berlumur racun oleh Abd al-Rahman ibn Muljam al-Muradi, seorang Khawarij, saat sedang menuju masjid untuk shalat Shubuh pada 17 Ramadhan 40 H / Januari 661 M. Ali bertahan dua hari sebelum wafat. Dengan wafatnya Ali, berakhirlah era Khulafaur Rasyidin, masa keemasan pemerintahan Islam yang langsung di bawah bimbingan nilai-nilai Nabi ﷺ.

title_en: Hasan ibn Ali's Abdication — End of the Rashidun Era
title_ar: تنازل الحسن بن علي — نهاية عهد الخلفاء الراشدين
description_en: After Ali's assassination, his son Hasan ibn Ali was given the pledge of allegiance as caliph by the people of Iraq. However, Muawiyah marched with the Syrian army and the two forces faced each other at Maskin on the Tigris. Hasan, seeking to end Muslim bloodshed, negotiated a treaty with Muawiyah under which he surrendered the caliphate in exchange for guarantees regarding governance and the rights of his family. The Prophet ﷺ had praised Hasan saying: 'My son here is a master, and through him Allah will reconcile two great factions of Muslims.' This year became known as 'Aam al-Jama'ah — the Year of Unity. The transfer of power to Muawiyah marked the beginning of the Umayyad Caliphate and the end of the Rashidun era — a golden period of Islamic governance characterised by shura, personal piety, and direct connection to the Prophet's example.
title_id: Hasan ibn Ali Mengundurkan Diri — Berakhirnya Era Rasyidin
description_id: Setelah pembunuhan Ali, putranya Hasan ibn Ali mendapatkan bai'at sebagai khalifah dari penduduk Irak dan Kufah. Namun dalam menghadapi pasukan Muawiyah yang besar dan demi menghindari pertumpahan darah lebih lanjut di antara kaum Muslimin, Hasan menyerahkan kekuasaan kepada Muawiyah pada 25 Rabi'ul Awal 41 H / 661 M dengan syarat-syarat tertentu. Tahun ini dikenal sebagai 'Am al-Jama'ah (Tahun Persatuan). Tindakan mulia Hasan ini dipuji oleh Nabi ﷺ yang telah memprediksinya.
